page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARMv8-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and is bundled with 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core execution. The stress-ng single-core CPU test registers an average score of 1519.49, whereas multi-core performance falls into the weak category at 3012.08. Memory bandwidth tests show 117.2 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 36.85 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance exhibits lower performance in compute-heavy tasks, as evidenced by poor scores in Geekbench multi-core (1753.0) and Passmark CPU (1349.04).

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r6gd.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage on an energy-efficient ARM64 architecture, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute parallelism. It is suitable for workloads such as caching layers, database operations, and lightweight web serving, though it is constrained by its 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and lower multi-threaded processing capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.49, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 1753.0. Qualitatively, the instance provides high memory density and local SSD storage on a cost-efficient ARM architecture. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations that do not demand high multi-threaded compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for caching and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores, high memory density, and local NVMe SSD storage for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8:1 memory-to-core ratio
Includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers average single-core CPU performance but weaker multi-core scaling
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching, and database operations
